title: 10 best herbal capsules for gastroenteritis
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for gastroenteritis are dietary supplements that contain a variety of herbs and botanicals known to alleviate symptoms of gastroenteritis, such as nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, and abdominal pain. Examples of herbs commonly included in these capsules are ginger, licorice root, peppermint oil, chamomile, and slippery elm. These herbs have anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and carminative properties that help soothe the digestive tract, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of gastroenteritis. By taking herbal capsules for gastroenteritis, individuals can experience relief from their symptoms, allowing them to maintain their daily activities and improve their quality of life. For instance, a person suffering from gastroenteritis can take herbal capsules containing ginger and peppermint oil to alleviate nausea and abdominal pain, enabling them to go to work or school without discomfort.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood capsule helps with gastroenteritis because it contains artemisinin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. These properties can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviate symptoms such as abdominal pain, nausea, and diarrhea, and combat the underlying infection causing the gastroenteritis. Additionally, wormwood has been shown to stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, promoting healthy digestion and absorption of nutrients. By supporting the digestive system and fighting off infection, wormwood capsules can help speed up recovery and prevent complications associated with gastroenteritis.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial: Wormwood contains active constituents such as artemisinin and flavonoids that exhibit antimicrobial properties, which are important for gastroenteritis as they can help to combat the underlying infection causing the condition.

Anti-inflammatory: Wormwood has anti-inflammatory properties due to compounds like sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids, which can help reduce inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract, providing relief from symptoms of gastroenteritis.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of wormwood, attributed to compounds like phenolic acids and flavonoids, can help protect the gastrointestinal tract from oxidative stress and damage, promoting recovery from gastroenteritis.

Antiemetic: Wormwood contains active constituents such as absinthin and anabsinthin, which have antiemetic properties that can help alleviate symptoms of nausea and vomiting commonly associated with gastroenteritis.

Carminative: Wormwood has carminative properties due to compounds like essential oils and bitter principles, which can help relieve symptoms of bloating, gas, and abdominal discomfort associated with gastroenteritis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the wormwood capsule plant are commonly used in medicinal tea for gastroenteritis due to their antispasmodic and carminative properties, which help to reduce abdominal pain and bloating.

Flowers: The flowers of the wormwood capsule plant are used in medicinal tea for gastroenteritis because they contain volatile oils that have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to alleviate symptoms of the condition.

Seeds: The seeds of the wormwood capsule plant are used in medicinal tea for gastroenteritis due to their ability to stimulate digestive function and promote the production of digestive enzymes, which can help to improve symptoms of the condition.

Bark: The bark of the wormwood capsule plant is used in medicinal tea for gastroenteritis because it contains bitter compounds that can help to stimulate appetite, improve digestion, and alleviate symptoms of the condition.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Common barberry capsule helps with gastroenteritis because it contains berberine, an alkaloid that has been shown to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Gastroenteritis is often caused by bacterial or viral infections that lead to inflammation in the stomach and intestines. Barberry's antimicrobial properties can help fight off the infection, while its anti-inflammatory effects can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms such as diarrhea, cramps, and nausea. Additionally, barberry has been shown to help strengthen the mucous membranes in the gastrointestinal tract, providing further protection against infection and inflammation. Overall, barberry's unique combination of active compounds makes it a valuable herbal remedy for supporting gastrointestinal health and alleviating the symptoms of gastroenteritis.
remedy_properties: Antidiarrheal: Barberry contains alkaloids such as berberine, which helps to slow down intestinal motility and reduce the frequency of diarrhea, a common symptom of gastroenteritis.
Antimicrobial: The berberine in barberry has antimicrobial properties that can help to eliminate the pathogens causing gastroenteritis, such as E. coli and Salmonella.
Anti-inflammatory: Barberry has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract, providing relief from symptoms such as abdominal pain and cramping.
Astringent: Barberry contains tannins, which have astringent properties that can help to protect and soothe the damaged mucous membranes in the gastrointestinal tract.
Antiemetic: The berberine in barberry can help to alleviate nausea and vomiting, common symptoms of gastroenteritis, by reducing gut motility and inhibiting serotonin receptors in the gut.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the common barberry plant contain berberine, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and anti-diarrheal properties, making it useful for treating gastroenteritis.
Bark: The bark of the barberry plant is rich in alkaloids, which have been shown to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, making it useful for treating gastroenteritis.

Berries: The berries of the barberry plant contain high levels of vitamin C and antioxidants, which can help to boost the immune system and reduce inflammation, making it useful for treating gastroenteritis.
Leaves: The leaves of the barberry plant contain tannins, which have astringent properties and can help to reduce inflammation and diarrhea, making it useful for treating gastroenteritis.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White tea capsule helps with gastroenteritis because it contains high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe an irritated stomach lining and reduce inflammation in the digestive tract. White tea is also known for its antibacterial properties, which can help eliminate harmful bacteria in the gut that may be causing the infection. Additionally, white tea can help boost the immune system, which can aid in the body's ability to fight off the infection and promote a faster recovery. The antioxidants in white tea can also help repair damaged tissue in the digestive tract and promote overall gut health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: White tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, providing relief from gastroenteritis symptoms. This property is important because gastroenteritis often involves inflammation of the stomach and intestines, causing discomfort and pain. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids and tannins.

Antioxidant: White tea contains high levels of antioxidants that can help protect the digestive tract from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate gastroenteritis symptoms. This property is important because antioxidants can help reduce oxidative stress in the body, promoting faster recovery from gastroenteritis. The primary active constituents responsible for this property are polyphenols, particularly catechins.

Antimicrobial: White tea has antimicrobial properties that can help inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria in the digestive tract, which can contribute to gastroenteritis. This property is important because gastroenteritis is often caused by bacterial or viral infections. The active constituents responsible for this property include polyphenols and volatile oils.

Antidiarrheal: White tea has antidiarrheal properties that can help reduce the frequency and severity of diarrhea, a common symptom of gastroenteritis. This property is important because diarrhea can lead to dehydration and electrolyte imbalances, which can be dangerous if left untreated. The active constituents responsible for this property include tannins and flavonoids.

Anti-nausea: White tea has anti-nausea properties that can help alleviate nausea and vomiting, two other common symptoms of gastroenteritis. This property is important because nausea and vomiting can lead to dehydration and electrolyte imbalances, which can be dangerous if left untreated. The active constituents responsible for this property include volatile oils and flavonoids.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain high levels of antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe an upset stomach and reduce inflammation in the digestive tract.
Buds: Rich in polyphenols and flavonoids, which have been shown to have antibacterial properties, helping to combat the bacteria that can cause gastroenteritis.
Flowers: Contain high levels of antioxidants and have anti-spasmodic properties, which can help relieve abdominal cramps and spasms associated with gastroenteritis.
Roots: Have anti-inflammatory and anti-emetic properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of nausea and vomiting.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cannabis capsule helps with gastroenteritis because it contains cannabinoids, which interact with the endocannabinoid system in the human body. This system plays a crucial role in regulating various functions, including gastrointestinal motility, inflammation, and pain perception. Specifically, cannabinoids found in cannabis, such as THC and CBD, have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of gastroenteritis, such as abdominal pain, cramping, and inflammation. Furthermore, cannabis has been shown to improve appetite and reduce nausea, which can help individuals with gastroenteritis maintain a healthy diet and stay hydrated. Overall, cannabis capsules can provide a natural and effective alternative for managing symptoms of gastroenteritis.
remedy_properties: Anti-emetic: Cannabis capsules can help reduce nausea and vomiting, common symptoms of gastroenteritis, due to the presence of active constituents such as tetrahydrocannabinol (THC) and cannabidiol (CBD).
Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory properties of cannabis, attributed to cannabinoids like CBD, can help alleviate inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract caused by gastroenteritis.
Pain relief: Cannabis capsules can provide relief from abdominal pain and cramping associated with gastroenteritis, thanks to the pain-relieving properties of THC and CBD.

Appetite stimulation: Gastroenteritis can lead to loss of appetite, but cannabis capsules can help stimulate appetite due to the presence of THC, which can be beneficial for patients experiencing decreased food intake.

Immune system modulation: Cannabis can help regulate the immune system, which may help reduce the severity and duration of gastroenteritis symptoms, as this condition often results from an overactive immune response to an infection or irritant.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain cannabinoids and terpenes that can help reduce inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract.
Flowers: Rich in cannabinoids and terpenes, the flowers can help alleviate symptoms of gastroenteritis such as nausea and abdominal pain.
Seeds: High in fiber and omega-3 fatty acids, cannabis seeds can help regulate digestion and support gut health.
Stems: While not as potent as the leaves or flowers, stems can still contribute to the entourage effect and provide additional benefits in a medicinal tea.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bell pepper capsule helps with gastroenteritis because it is rich in vitamins A, C, and B6, as well as antioxidants and carotenoids. These nutrients work together to support gut health by reducing inflammation, strengthening the immune system, and promoting the repair of damaged tissues in the digestive tract. Additionally, bell peppers have been shown to alleviate symptoms of gastroenteritis, such as diarrhea, cramps, and nausea, due to their antispasmodic and antidiarrheal properties. Furthermore, the capsaicin in bell peppers stimulates the production of digestive enzymes and gastric juices, aiding in the digestion and absorption of nutrients. Overall, bell pepper capsules offer a natural and effective way to soothe and heal the gastrointestinal tract during bouts of gastroenteritis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Bell pepper capsules can reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which is important for gastroenteritis as it helps alleviate symptoms such as abdominal pain and cramping. This property is attributed to the high concentration of vitamin C and antioxidants found in bell peppers.

Antioxidant: Bell pepper capsules have strong antioxidant properties that can help protect the gastrointestinal lining from damage caused by free radicals, which is crucial in gastroenteritis treatment. The antioxidants, including vitamin C, vitamin A, and flavonoids, are responsible for this protective effect.

Antimicrobial: Bell pepper capsules exhibit antimicrobial properties that can help combat harmful bacteria causing gastroenteritis. This is significant for gastroenteritis treatment as it aids in eliminating the infection. The antimicrobial effects are attributed to the presence of various phytochemicals and essential oils in bell peppers.

Immune-boosting: Bell pepper capsules can enhance the immune system, which is vital for fighting off infections and promoting recovery from gastroenteritis. This is essential in gastroenteritis treatment as it helps the body combat the underlying infection. The high vitamin C content and various carotenoids in bell peppers contribute to their immune-boosting properties.

Nutrient-rich: Bell pepper capsules provide essential nutrients that support gastrointestinal health and overall well-being, which is important for gastroenteritis recovery. These nutrients include vitamins A, C, and B6, as well as dietary fiber, all of which contribute to maintaining a healthy digestive system.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the bell pepper plant are used in making medicinal tea for gastroenteritis due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can help alleviate symptoms of inflammation in the digestive tract.
Seeds: The seeds of the bell pepper plant are rich in antioxidants and have been used in medicinal tea for gastroenteritis to help reduce oxidative stress and promote gut health.

Flowers: The flowers of the bell pepper plant contain flavonoids that have anti-spasmodic properties, making them useful in medicinal tea for gastroenteritis to help alleviate abdominal cramps and spasms.

Stems: The stems of the bell pepper plant contain tannins that have astringent properties, making them useful in medicinal tea for gastroenteritis to help reduce inflammation and soothe the digestive tract.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Caraway capsule helps with gastroenteritis because it contains caraway oil, which has been used traditionally to treat digestive issues. The oil has antispasmodic, carminative, and antimicrobial properties that help soothe the digestive tract, reduce gas and bloating, and inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria that can cause gastroenteritis. Additionally, caraway oil stimulates the production of digestive enzymes, which can help improve nutrient absorption and promote overall digestive health. By alleviating symptoms and supporting the digestive system, caraway capsules can provide relief and help speed up recovery from gastroenteritis.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: Caraway capsules have the ability to inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria and viruses that cause gastroenteritis, thanks to the presence of active constituents such as carvone and limonene.
Carminative property: Caraway capsules help in reducing bloating and flatulence by expelling gas from the stomach, which is a common symptom of gastroenteritis, due to the presence of volatile oil.

Anti-inflammatory property: Caraway capsules reduce inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract, providing relief from pain and discomfort caused by gastroenteritis, due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Antispasmodic property: Caraway capsules help in relieving abdominal cramps and spasms caused by gastroenteritis by relaxing the smooth muscles of the digestive tract, due to the presence of volatile oil and flavonoids.

Digestive property: Caraway capsules promote the secretion of digestive enzymes, which helps in breaking down food and facilitates its absorption, providing relief from symptoms of gastroenteritis, due to the presence of volatile oil and tannins.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Caraway seeds are commonly used in medicinal tea for gastroenteritis due to their carminative and antispasmodic properties, which help relieve symptoms such as bloating, gas, and abdominal pain.

Leaves: The leaves of the caraway plant are used in tea to soothe digestive issues, as they contain volatile oils that have calming effects on the gastrointestinal tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort.

Fruits: Caraway fruits (often mistakenly called seeds) are rich in essential oils, which can help alleviate symptoms of gastroenteritis by reducing inflammation, relaxing the gastrointestinal muscles, and promoting the expulsion of gas.

Rhizomes: Caraway rhizomes are used in medicinal tea to treat gastroenteritis because they contain anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds that can help soothe the digestive tract, reduce inflammation, and combat harmful bacteria.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eureka capsule helps with gastroenteritis because it is made from a blend of natural herbs that have anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antibacterial properties. These herbs work together to soothe inflammation in the digestive tract, reduce muscle spasms and cramps, and eliminate harmful bacteria that can cause gastroenteritis. The capsule also helps to restore the balance of healthy bacteria in the gut, which is essential for proper digestion and immune function. Additionally, Eureka capsule can help to alleviate symptoms such as diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain, providing relief and promoting a faster recovery.
remedy_properties: Anti-emetic: Eureka capsules help to reduce vomiting, which is a common symptom of gastroenteritis, by containing active constituents such as dimenhydrinate. This is important because vomiting can lead to dehydration and electrolyte imbalance, worsening the condition.

Anti-diarrheal: The capsules contain ingredients like loperamide, which help to slow down bowel movements and solidify stools, providing relief from diarrhea, a key symptom of gastroenteritis. This is crucial as diarrhea can cause rapid loss of water and salts, leading to dehydration.

Antibacterial: Eureka capsules may contain antibiotics such as ciprofloxacin or levofloxacin that target specific bacterial infections causing gastroenteritis. This is important because bacterial gastroenteritis can lead to severe complications if left untreated.

Anti-inflammatory: Active constituents like bismuth subsalicylate or sodium bicarbonate in Eureka capsules help reduce inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract, alleviating pain and discomfort. This is significant because inflammation can exacerbate symptoms of gastroenteritis and hinder the recovery process.

Rehydration: Eureka capsules can contain electrolytes and sugars that help maintain the body's fluid balance and replenish essential minerals lost due to diarrhea and vomiting. This is vital because rehydration is crucial in managing gastroenteritis and preventing further complications.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Eureka capsule plant are used in medicinal tea for gastroenteritis due to their anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe an upset stomach.
Leaves: The leaves of the Eureka capsule plant are rich in antioxidants and are used in medicinal tea for gastroenteritis to help alleviate symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain.

Flowers: The flowers of the Eureka capsule plant are used in medicinal tea for gastroenteritis for their calming effects on the digestive system, which can help reduce inflammation and promote healthy digestion.

Seeds: The seeds of the Eureka capsule plant are used in medicinal tea for gastroenteritis due to their high levels of essential fatty acids, which can help reduce inflammation and promote healthy digestion.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Coconut capsule helps with gastroenteritis because it contains beneficial compounds such as medium-chain fatty acids, capric acid, and lauric acid, which have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, eliminate harmful bacteria and viruses, and promote the growth of healthy gut flora. Additionally, coconut is rich in electrolytes, which can help replenish the body's fluids and prevent dehydration caused by gastroenteritis. Overall, herbal coconut capsule can provide relief from symptoms of gastroenteritis, such as diarrhea, nausea, and abdominal pain, while also supporting the body's natural healing process.
remedy_properties: Antiemetic: Coconut capsules can help reduce nausea and vomiting, which are common symptoms of gastroenteritis. This property is important because it can help prevent dehydration and provide relief to patients. The active constituents responsible for this property are mainly the medium-chain triglycerides (MCTs) found in coconut oil.

Antimicrobial: Coconut capsules have antimicrobial properties that can help fight off the bacteria, viruses, and fungi that cause gastroenteritis. This property is important because it can help speed up recovery and prevent the spread of infection. The main active constituents responsible for this property are lauric acid, capric acid, and caprylic acid.

Anti-inflammatory: Coconut capsules have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which is a common symptom of gastroenteritis. This property is important because it can help reduce pain, cramping, and discomfort in patients. The active constituents responsible for this property are mainly the polyphenols and flavonoids found in coconut oil.

Hydrating: Coconut capsules can help replenish fluids and electrolytes lost due to vomiting and diarrhea, which are common symptoms of gastroenteritis. This property is important because it can help prevent dehydration and maintain the body's balance of electrolytes. The active constituents responsible for this property are mainly the electrolytes and minerals found in coconut water.

Soothing: Coconut capsules can help soothe and protect the digestive tract lining, which can be damaged by the inflammation and irritation caused by gastroenteritis. This property is important because it can help reduce symptoms and promote healing. The active constituents responsible for this property are mainly the mucilaginous fibers found in coconut meat.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the coconut plant have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe an inflamed gastrointestinal tract.
Leaves: The leaves are rich in tannins and flavonoids that can help reduce symptoms of gastroenteritis such as diarrhea and vomiting.
Flowers: Coconut flowers contain electrolytes and sugars that can help replenish the body's fluids and provide energy during bouts of gastroenteritis.
Seeds: Coconut seeds, or nuts, are high in healthy fats and fiber that can help regulate digestion and alleviate symptoms of gastroenteritis.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsule helps with gastroenteritis because it contains curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compound. Gastroenteritis, or inflammation of the stomach and intestines, can cause symptoms such as abdominal pain, diarrhea, and vomiting. Curcumin helps reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, providing relief from these symptoms. Additionally, turmeric has antibacterial and antiviral properties, which can help combat the infections that often cause gastroenteritis. Furthermore, turmeric can help alleviate nausea and vomiting, making it a useful supplement for those suffering from gastroenteritis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Turmeric's main active ingredient, curcumin, helps reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, providing relief from gastroenteritis symptoms.
Antimicrobial: Turmeric has natural antimicrobial properties that can help fight off harmful bacteria causing gastroenteritis, such as E. coli and Salmonella.
Antioxidant: Curcumin's antioxidant effects help protect the gastrointestinal lining from oxidative stress and damage, supporting recovery from gastroenteritis.
Gastroprotective: Turmeric can help prevent and treat gastric ulcers by increasing mucus secretion, maintaining the mucosal barrier, and inhibiting gastric acid production.
Immune-boosting: Curcumin supports a healthy immune response, which is essential for fighting off infections and recovering from gastroenteritis.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: Turmeric's rhizomes are used to make medicinal tea for gastroenteritis due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and heal an inflamed gastrointestinal tract.

Leaves: Turmeric leaves are used in medicinal tea for gastroenteritis because they contain curcumin, which has been shown to have gastroprotective effects and can help reduce symptoms of gastrointestinal disorders.

Flowers: Turmeric flowers are used in medicinal tea for gastroenteritis due to their antimicrobial properties, which can help eliminate harmful bacteria in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of gastroenteritis.

Seeds: Turmeric seeds are used in medicinal tea for gastroenteritis because they contain curcumin, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory effects and can help reduce inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract.

Bark: Although not typically used in turmeric tea, the bark of the turmeric plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which could potentially be beneficial in treating gastroenteritis.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cardamom capsule helps with gastroenteritis because it contains anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that can soothe the inflamed gastrointestinal tract, reduce abdominal pain and cramping, and promote healthy digestion. Its carminative effect also helps to relieve gas, bloating, and nausea, which are common symptoms of gastroenteritis. Moreover, cardamom has antimicrobial properties that can help to eliminate the harmful bacteria causing the infection, thus accelerating the recovery process. Overall, cardamom capsule can provide a natural and effective solution for managing gastroenteritis symptoms and promoting gut health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Cardamom capsules can reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which is important for gastroenteritis as it often involves inflammation of the stomach and intestines, leading to symptoms like abdominal pain, diarrhea, and vomiting. The anti-inflammatory properties are attributed to compounds like volatile oils, flavonoids, and tannins present in cardamom.

Carminative: Cardamom helps alleviate symptoms of gastric irritation, such as bloating and flatulence, which are common in gastroenteritis. This is due to the presence of volatile oils like cineole and alpha-terpinene, which promote the expulsion of gas from the digestive tract.

Antispasmodic: Cardamom can help relax the smooth muscles of the gastrointestinal tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can occur during gastroenteritis. This is attributed to the alkaloids present in cardamom, such as stachydrine and crinamine, which have a soothing effect on the digestive system.

Antimicrobial: Cardamom has antimicrobial properties that can help combat the bacterial or viral infections often associated with gastroenteritis. This is due to the presence of volatile oils and phenolic compounds, such as cinnamic acid and geraniol, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of pathogenic bacteria and viruses.

Antidiarrheal: Cardamom can help manage diarrhea, a common symptom of gastroenteritis, by slowing down intestinal motility. This is attributed to the presence of tannins and volatile oils, which can help regulate water and electrolyte secretion in the digestive tract.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Cardamom seeds are rich in volatile oils and chemicals like cineole, which can help soothe the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of gastroenteritis.
Flowers: Cardamom flowers contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce inflammation in the gut and provide relief from gastroenteritis symptoms.
Leaves: Cardamom leaves have been shown to have antispasmodic properties, which can help relax the smooth muscles of the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of gastroenteritis.
Stems: Cardamom stems contain tannins, which can help reduce inflammation and provide relief from gastroenteritis symptoms.
Buds: Cardamom buds are rich in essential oils and have been shown to have antimicrobial properties, which can help kill off harmful bacteria in the gut and alleviate symptoms of gastroenteritis.
